questions.question_text: Select a figure from amongst the Answer Figures which will continue the same series as established by the five Problem Figures.
questions.solution: Two and three half-leaves are added to the figure alternately. The addition of half-leaves takes place in an anti-clockwise direction.

questions.question_text: Select a figure from amongst the Answer Figures which will continue the same series as established by the five Problem Figures.
questions.solution: The V sign moves two spaces (each space is equal to half-a-side of the square boundary) and one space anti-clockwise alternately. The '=' sign moves one space and two spaces anti-clockwise alternately.

questions.question_text: Select a figure from amongst the Answer Figures which will continue the same series as established by the five Problem Figures.
questions.solution: In each step, the first element moves to the third position and gets replaced by a new element; the second and the third elements move to the first and the second positions respectively and the entire figure rotates 90o clockwise.

questions.solution:

questions.question_text: Find the missing character, if a certain rule is followed either row-wise or column-wise.
questions.solution:
The rule followed is:

{tex}\begin{aligned} &\mathrm{B} \stackrel{+2}{\longrightarrow} \mathrm{D} \stackrel{+4}{\longrightarrow} \mathrm{H}, \mathrm{G} \stackrel{+3}{\longrightarrow} \mathrm{J} \stackrel{+6}{\longrightarrow} \mathrm{P},\mathrm{N} \stackrel{+4}{\longrightarrow} \mathrm{R} \stackrel{\mathrm{Z}}{\longrightarrow} \mathrm{Z} \end{aligned}{/tex}

questions.question_text: Select a figure from amongst the figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) which will continue the series as established by the five problem figures.
questions.solution: The symbol gets vertically inverted and laterally inverted alternately. It also moves in anti-clockwise direction through distances equal to 1 sides and {tex}1\frac 12{/tex} sides alternately.

questions.question_text: Select a figure from amongst the figures (A), (B), (C) and (D) which will continue the series as established by the five problem figures.
Find the missing number, if same rule is followed in all the three figures.
questions.solution:
The rule followed:

(5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) - (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3) = 25 + 4 - 9 = 20;

(6 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 + 9 {tex}\times{/tex} 9) - (10 {tex}\times{/tex} 10) = 36 + 81 - 100 = 17

So, (12 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 + 13 {tex}\times{/tex} 13) - (8 {tex}\times{/tex} 8)

= 144 + 169 - 64 = 249

questions.question_text: Find the missing character, if same rule is followed in all the three figures.
questions.solution: R

questions.question_text: Find the missing number, if a certain rule is followed either row-wise or column-wise.
questions.solution:
The rule followed:

(42 - 38) {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 28;

(28 - 23) {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 35

So, (39 - 37) {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 14

questions.question_text: Find the number which will replace the ? mark.
questions.solution:
In each row, last term = sum of first two terms

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Missing term = 3 + {tex}\frac34{/tex} = {tex}\frac{15}4{/tex}

questions.question_text: Find the number which will replace the ? mark.
questions.solution:
Lower number = (Product of upper numbers) {tex}\times{/tex} 2

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required number = (1 {tex}\times{/tex} 5) {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 10

questions.question_text: Find the number which will replace the ? mark.
questions.solution: The rule followed is, 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 + 3 = 45, 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 + 6 = 26, 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 + 8 = 29

questions.question_text: Find the number which will replace the ? mark.
questions.solution:
The rule followed,

{tex}\frac{5 \times 6 \times 4}{10}{/tex} = 12; {tex}\frac{6 \times 7 \times 5}{10}{/tex} = 21

So, missing number = {tex}\frac{4 \times 8 \times 10}{10}{/tex} = 32

questions.question_text: Find the number which will replace the ? mark.
questions.solution:
Multiply the end numbers of each box and then add the results to get number in the centre.

So, the missing number = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 + 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 82

questions.question_text: Find the number which will replace the ? mark.
Find the number which will replace the ? mark.
questions.solution: The number inside the circle is equal to the difference between the sum of the numbers at left and right & sum of the numbers at the top and bottom.

questions.question_text: Find the number which will replace the ? mark.
questions.solution: Clearly, sum of numbers in each row is 17. So, missing number = 17 - (4 + 7) = 6

questions.question_text: Find the number which will replace the ? mark.
questions.solution: Lower number = {tex}\frac {\text{Product of upper numbers}} 6{/tex}

questions.question_text: Find the number which will replace the ? mark.
Select a figure from the options which will continue the same series as established by the Problem Figures.
Find the missing number.
questions.solution:
The rule followed is:

3 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 = 90; 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 360

So, 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 64

questions.question_text: Select a figure from the options which will continue the same series as established by the Problem Figures.
questions.solution: In every next figure, the figure rotates 90°, 45°, 135°, 45°, 180° clockwise.

questions.question_text: Find the missing letter, if a certain rule is followed either row-wise or column-wise.
Select a figure from the options which will continue the same series as established by the Problem Figures.
Find the number which will continue the given number pattern.
Find the missing number, if same rule is followed in all the three figures.
questions.solution:
The rule followed is:

(5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5) - (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2)

= 125 - 27 - 8 = 90

(10 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 10) - (9 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 + 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 6)

= 1000 - 729 - 216 = 55

So, (21 {tex}\times{/tex} 21 {tex}\times{/tex} 21) - (12 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 + 13 {tex}\times{/tex} 13 {tex}\times{/tex} 13)

= 9261 - 1728 - 2197 = 5336

questions.question_text: Select a figure from the options which will replace the (?) in the series as established by the Problem Figures.
Find the missing number, if same rule is followed in all the three figures.
questions.solution:
The rule followed is:

(4 {tex}\times{/tex} 3) - 5 = 7; (8 {tex}\times{/tex} 7) - 3 = 53

So, (5 {tex}\times{/tex} 4) - 5 = 15

questions.question_text: Find the missing number, if same rule is followed in all the three figures.
questions.solution: In each figure, all the four numbers outside the box are divisible by the number inside the box.

questions.question_text: Find the missing character, if same rule is followed in all the three figures.
questions.solution: K

questions.question_text: Select a figure from the options which will replace the question mark to complete the given series.
questions.solution: Each element moves one step forward in the anti-clockwise direction.

questions.question_text: There is a series of five figures. In order to continue the series, choose a figure from the options that could be the next term of the series.
questions.solution: In each next step, the pin rotates 90oCW and the arrow rotates 90o ACW.

questions.question_text: There is a series of five figures. In order to continue the series, choose a figure from the options that could be the next term of the series.
questions.solution: Each element moves {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} step clockwise.

questions.question_text: There is a series of five figures. In order to continue the series, choose a figure from the options that could be the next term of the series.
questions.solution: The outer element rotates 45o clockwise (CW) and 90o CW alternately. The inner element rotates 90o CW.

questions.question_text: There is a series of five figures. In order to continue the series, choose a figure from the options that could be the next term of the series.
questions.solution: In each next step, the figure  rotates 90o CW and 45o CW alternately.

questions.question_text: There is a series of problem figures with one figure missing. Choose the missing figure from the four options.
questions.solution: In each step, the cross moves to the adjacent corner (of the square) in ACW direction and the circle moves to the adjacent corner in CW direction.

questions.question_text: There is a series of problem figures with one figure missing. Choose the missing figure from the four options.
questions.solution: {tex}\triangle{/tex} and  are moving CW while {tex}\bullet{/tex} and {tex}\square{/tex} are moving ACW in each step.

questions.question_text: There is a series of problem figures with one figure missing. Choose the missing figure from the four options.
questions.solution: In each step, the central element (comprising of the circle and a radius) rotates 90oCW while the small black circle moves to the adjacent corner of the square in ACW direction.

questions.question_text: Select a figure from the options which will continue the same series as established by the problem Figures.
Which of the following figures will complete the given figure series?
questions.solution: Every element moves one block in clockwise direction in each next step. Also, square and circle gets shaded and unshaded alternately.

questions.question_text: Select a figure from the options which will continue the same series as established by the Problem Figures.
questions.solution: In each next step, a new element is added to the right of previous element(s) and previous element(s) is(are) flipped horizontally.

questions.question_text: Select a figure from the options which will continue the same series as established by the Problem Figures.
questions.solution: Upper left corner and lower right corner element moves one step anti-clockwise along the corners. Upper right corner and lower left corner element moves one step clockwise along the corners. Also, the element in the centre increases by +1, +2, +3, +4, ...

questions.question_text: Select a figure from amongst the options which will continue the series established by the five problem figures.
questions.solution: Each of 3 elements in the circle moves one step anti-clockwise. Also each element changes its position inward and outwards.

questions.question_text: Select a figure from amongst the options which will continue the series established by the five problem figures.
questions.solution: In each next step 1 dot diminish and 1 x added in place of other dot.

questions.question_text: Select a figure from amongst the options which will continue the series established by the five problem figures.
questions.solution: Shade in small square moves 1 step, 2 steps, 3 steps, ... anti-clockwise. Shade in large square moves 1 step, 2 steps, 3 steps, ... clockwise. Also both squares move one step in anti-clockwise direction.

questions.question_text: Choose the missing figure from the options.
questions.solution: White circle moves 2 steps anticlockwise in each next figure.

questions.question_text: Choose the missing figure from the options.
questions.solution: In each next term, the black circle moves three steps clockwise and the white circle moves two steps clockwise.

questions.question_text: Choose the missing figure from the options.
questions.solution: In each step, the four elements on the circumference of the large circle interchange positions in an anti-clockwise direction, the only element that reaches the topmost position gets shaded.

questions.question_text: Find the missing figure which will replace the (?) in Problem Figures to complete the series.
questions.solution: In every next figure, the arrow moves 90o clockwise. Also, dot moves 2 steps, 3 steps,... in every next step.
